signal_catalog.update_frequency: Near real-time (<60 seconds URT for US/Canada, <60 minutes standard)
signal_catalog.auth_details: Free MAP_KEY required, 5000 requests per 10-minute window
signal_catalog.notes: Covers MODIS, VIIRS S-NPP, VIIRS NOAA-20, VIIRS NOAA-21, Landsat. URT data available in <60s for US/Canada. US/Canada-specific endpoint at firms.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/usfs/. 375m VIIRS resolution, 1km MODIS.

signal_catalog.format: ArcGIS REST, GeoJSON, Shapefile, KML, CSV, FGDB
signal_catalog.auth_details: No login required
signal_catalog.notes: Replaced GeoMAC in May 2020. WFIGS authoritative wildfire perimeters. ArcGIS REST services for current and historic perimeters (2000-present). Hosted by NIFC, scales to load.

signal_catalog.source_name: IRWIN (Integrated Reporting of Wildland Fire Information)
signal_catalog.api_endpoint: https://data-nifc.opendata.arcgis.com/ (public view); ArcGIS REST services (connected systems)
signal_catalog.format: ArcGIS REST, GeoJSON, Shapefile, CSV, KML
signal_catalog.auth_details: Public view via NIFC Open Data (no auth). Full API requires GeoPlatform token (federal/agency credential). Token max lifetime 60 minutes.
signal_catalog.notes: Authoritative wildfire incident data since May 2014. v11 API specification available (2025). Public view provides location, size, conditions. Full connected-system access requires NWCG affiliation. Export formats include GeoJSON.

signal_catalog.auth_details: Free API token required. NOAA users can access NMP data.
signal_catalog.notes: Access to 170,000+ stations across 320+ networks worldwide including all RAWS stations. Key endpoints: /stations/timeseries, /stations/latest, /stations/precipitation. SynopticPy Python client available. Legacy endpoint at api.mesowest.net/v2/.

signal_catalog.auth_details: Public open data on AWS, Google, and Azure. No subscription required.
signal_catalog.notes: 3km resolution, convection-allowing atmospheric model. Rolling 1-week archive in noaa-hrrr-pds, full archive in noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds. Forecasts to 48h at 00/06/12/19 UTC, 18h otherwise. SNS notifications available. HRRR-B Python package for data access.

signal_catalog.update_frequency: Day 1-3: every 30 minutes; Day 4-7: every 6 hours
signal_catalog.auth_details: Public, no API key. Rate limit: 1 request per point per hour.
signal_catalog.notes: Gridded forecast data from WFOs and NCEP. REST and SOAP services. Supports queries by zip code, lat/lon, and bounding box. WMS endpoints for CONUS, Alaska, Hawaii, PR, Guam. Also available on AWS Open Data.

signal_catalog.source_name: NWS Alerts API (incl. Red Flag Warnings)
signal_catalog.update_frequency: Real-time (alerts published within 45 seconds via NOAAPORT)
signal_catalog.auth_details: Free, open data. Generous rate limits. User-Agent header recommended.
signal_catalog.notes: Covers all NWS watches, warnings, advisories. Filter Red Flag Warnings with ?event=Red%20Flag%20Warning. Query by state (?area=CA), point, zone. Fire weather event codes: FWW (warning), FWA (watch), RFD (rangeland/grassland). 7-day historical via /alerts.

signal_catalog.auth_details: Public STAC API, no auth for search. AWS cloud access for imagery.
signal_catalog.notes: 30m resolution multispectral imagery from 1972-present. STAC metadata with direct S3 links. Also available via Element 84 Earth Search (earth-search.aws.element84.com/v1). PySTAC Python client. Supports spatial and temporal filtering.

signal_catalog.auth_details: STAC search is public. Download may require Copernicus account (free).
signal_catalog.notes: 10m/20m/60m resolution multispectral. L2A (surface reflectance) and L1C products. CQL2 filter extension. Also available via Element 84 Earth Search. STAC Browser at browser.stac.dataspace.copernicus.eu. Old endpoint (catalogue.dataspace.copernicus.eu/stac) deprecated Nov 2025.

signal_catalog.update_frequency: Annual (assessments for fires >1000 acres west, >500 acres east; rapid assessments for >40k acres since 2020)
signal_catalog.format: GeoTIFF, Shapefile, Google Earth Engine, Planetary Computer STAC
signal_catalog.auth_details: Free public data, no authentication required.
signal_catalog.notes: 30m burn severity mapping from Landsat (1984-present). Available on GEE, Microsoft Planetary Computer, USFS ArcGIS Hub, and Data.gov. Thematic classes: unburned/low, low, moderate, high, increased greenness. Critical for post-fire recovery planning.

signal_catalog.auth_details: Public REST API, no authentication required.
signal_catalog.notes: Vegetation, fuel, fire regime, and topographic data layers. RESTful API supports resampling, reprojection, and custom AOI extraction. R package (rlandfire) available. WMS and WCS also available for direct consumption. Critical for fuel modeling.

signal_catalog.auth_details: Google Cloud project required with Earth Engine API enabled. Service account + private key for REST API.
signal_catalog.notes: 80+ petabytes of geospatial data. Server-side computation platform. Includes Landsat, MODIS, Sentinel, MTBS, LANDFIRE, and hundreds more. REST API available but primarily designed for cloud-side computation, not direct data download. JavaScript/Python client libraries. Best used for derived products.

signal_catalog.auth_details: Free API key required (register at docs.airnowapi.org).
signal_catalog.notes: Real-time AQI data and forecasts. Query by zip code or lat/lon. Covers PM2.5, PM10, Ozone, NO2, CO, SO2. Critical for smoke impact assessment during wildfires. Simple REST interface.

signal_catalog.update_frequency: Historical (6+ month lag from collection to availability)
signal_catalog.auth_details: Free registration required (email validation). Rate limit: 10 requests/minute with 5-second pause between.
signal_catalog.notes: 3.4 billion rows of historical ambient air monitoring data. Supports sync and async queries. Available by site, county, state, CBSA, or bounding box. Python (pyaqsapi) and R (RAQSAPI) client libraries. Not real-time; use AirNow for current data.

signal_catalog.auth_details: Free API key required (linked to Google account). Points-based system; 1M points on signup, additional for purchase.
signal_catalog.notes: Dense network of low-cost PM2.5 sensors. Dual laser counter for data integrity. Historical data available. Extremely valuable for hyperlocal smoke monitoring during wildfires. Higher spatial density than AirNow in many areas. R and Python client libraries available.

signal_catalog.update_frequency: Real-time (15-minute intervals, transmitted hourly; more frequent during floods)
signal_catalog.auth_details: Public API, no authentication required.
signal_catalog.notes: 8,500+ continuous streamgages in National Streamflow Network. Streamflow, gage height, and hundreds of parameters. Modernized API also at api.waterdata.usgs.gov/. Critical for post-fire flood risk monitoring. RTFI (Real-Time Flood Impact) API also available.

signal_catalog.auth_details: Public API, no authentication required.
signal_catalog.notes: WARNING: Legacy StreamStats Services decommissioned Jan 30, 2026. Replaced by SS-Delineate and SS-Hydro. Provides watershed delineation, basin characteristics, peak flow estimates. Includes runoff modeling (Rational Method, TR55 Curve Number).

signal_catalog.source_name: NOAA National Water Prediction Service API
signal_catalog.auth_details: Public API, no authentication. Not supported 24/7; may be modified without notice.
signal_catalog.notes: Official NWS streamflow forecasts, observations, National Water Model output, crest history, flood impacts, flood category levels. HEFS ensemble forecasts for 3,000+ locations. Complements USGS raw streamflow data.

signal_catalog.source_name: USGS Post-Fire Debris Flow Hazard Assessments
signal_catalog.auth_details: Public ArcGIS MapServer, no authentication.
signal_catalog.notes: Probability and volume estimates for post-fire debris flows by watershed segment. ArcGIS Dashboard at arcgis.com/apps/dashboards/c09fa874362e48a9afe79432f2efe6fe. Landslides portal at landslides.usgs.gov/hazards/postfire_debrisflow/. Wildcat CLI tool available. Critical for post-fire safety.

signal_catalog.auth_details: Public data, no authentication.
signal_catalog.notes: National landslide inventory and susceptibility map using 3DEP elevation data. Hundreds of thousands of landslide locations. No dedicated REST API; data distributed via web maps, GIS file downloads, and ScienceBase. Less real-time utility.

signal_catalog.source_name: FIA (Forest Inventory and Analysis) API / EVALIDator
signal_catalog.update_frequency: Annual (state-level panel data, rolling inventory cycle)
signal_catalog.auth_details: Public API, no authentication required.
signal_catalog.notes: 130,000+ permanent forest inventory plots. Population estimates for timber volume, species composition, growth, mortality, biomass. Query by state or circular AOI around a point. Parameter dictionary at /fullreport/parameters/{param}. FIA DataMart for raw data downloads.

signal_catalog.api_endpoint: https://www.mrlc.gov/data (viewer/WMS); AWS S3 us-west-2; Planetary Computer STAC
signal_catalog.auth_details: Public data, no authentication. Planetary Computer provides SAS tokens.
signal_catalog.notes: 30m land cover classification for CONUS (1985-2024). Six annual products: land cover, change, confidence, impervious surface, descriptor, spectral change. Available on EarthExplorer, MRLC Viewer, ScienceBase, AWS S3, Planetary Computer. Tree Canopy Cover dataset included.

signal_catalog.update_frequency: 16-day composites; NRT via LANCE within 24 hours
signal_catalog.auth_details: NASA Earthdata account required (free).
signal_catalog.notes: MOD13Q1 (250m), MOD13A1 (500m), MYD13A2 (1km) NDVI products. AppEEARS provides area/point extraction and quality decoding. LANCE NRT NDVI for CONUS within 24h of observation. Continuity with AVHRR NDVI time series.

signal_catalog.update_frequency: Near real-time (updated by PIOs as incidents evolve)
signal_catalog.auth_details: Public, no authentication.
signal_catalog.notes: Public-facing incident information: personnel, cost, acres, containment, structures threatened. Drupal CMS since Oct 2022 at inciweb.wildfire.gov. No formal REST API; RSS feeds and KML are primary machine-readable options. Third-party scraper: github.com/datadesk/inciweb-wildfires.

signal_catalog.auth_details: Public API, no key required. CORS enabled. JSONP supported.
signal_catalog.notes: All Federal Register content since 1994. Search by agency, type, keyword. Includes Executive Orders, rules, proposed rules, notices. Endpoints for single doc, multi-get, search, public inspection. HTML/PDF/text versions. R client library available.

signal_catalog.auth_details: Public web portal, no authentication.
signal_catalog.notes: Lists all NEPA projects by forest. No documented REST API; data available through web portal and PDF reports. Would require scraping for MCP integration. ePlanning (BLM) has ArcGIS REST endpoints at gis.blm.gov/arcgis/rest/services/ePlanning/ but is BLM-specific.

signal_catalog.update_frequency: Continuous (updated as NEPA documents are published)
signal_catalog.auth_details: Public ArcGIS FeatureServer for geospatial queries.
signal_catalog.notes: Redesigned NEPA Register launched January 2026. ArcGIS REST services for geospatial project data. ePlanning UI at eplanning.blm.gov. BLM-specific (not USFS). Supports polygon, query, and statistics operations.

signal_catalog.source_name: CoCoRaHS (Community Collaborative Rain, Hail & Snow Network)
signal_catalog.auth_details: Public data access.
signal_catalog.notes: 27,500+ active volunteer observers across US, Canada, Bahamas. High-density precipitation measurements. Data Explorer (DEx) for browsing/comparison. NWS-approved rain gauges. Maps at maps.cocorahs.org. Useful for post-fire precipitation monitoring.

signal_catalog.agency: California Academy of Sciences / National Geographic
signal_catalog.auth_details: Read access is public (no key). Write access requires OAuth. Pagination limit: 10k results.
signal_catalog.notes: Biodiversity observations with species identification. v1 API recommended (Node-based, faster). v2 in development. Filter by taxon, location, date. Useful for post-fire ecological recovery monitoring. pyinaturalist Python client available. Bulk access via id_above pagination.

signal_catalog.agency: University of Nevada Reno / UCSD / University of Oregon
signal_catalog.update_frequency: Real-time (live camera feeds, panoramic scans every 2 minutes)
signal_catalog.format: Video streams (MJPEG/RTSP), no documented REST API
signal_catalog.auth_details: Public view of live feeds. Credentialed access for PTZ control, historical timelapse, and AI detection alerts. Credentials via wildfirehelp@uoregon.edu.
signal_catalog.notes: 1,600+ cameras in western US (ALERTCalifornia 1,200+, Oregon 70+, Nevada expanding). AI/ML fire detection every 2 minutes. Near-IR night vision. No public API documented; would require partnership for data feed access. ALERTWest.live aggregates all feeds.

tier_1_priority_sources: NASA FIRMS (MODIS + VIIRS) - Real-time fire detections with <60s latency, simple REST API, free key
NIFC Open Data / IRWIN - Authoritative wildfire perimeters and incident data, ArcGIS REST, public access
NWS Alerts API - Real-time Red Flag Warnings and fire weather alerts, JSON-LD, no auth required
USGS Water Services - Real-time streamflow for post-fire flood risk, 15-min intervals, no auth
USGS Post-Fire Debris Flow Assessments - Direct post-fire safety relevance, ArcGIS REST, public
AirNow + PurpleAir APIs - Complementary smoke/AQI monitoring (official + hyperlocal), simple REST
Synoptic Data / MesoWest (RAWS) - Fire weather station data, 170k+ stations, JSON API

signal_fusion_opportunities.intelligence_product: Combine active fire detections with real-time weather station data, Red Flag Warnings, and HRRR wind forecasts to predict fire spread direction and rate. Neither fire detection nor weather data alone provides spread prediction.

Overlay burn severity maps with debris flow probability models and real-time stream gauge data to create dynamic post-fire flood/debris cascade risk maps. Individual sources show static risk or current conditions; fusion creates predictive situational awareness.

Combine fire locations with official AQI, hyperlocal PurpleAir PM2.5, wind forecasts, and NWS air quality alerts to create real-time smoke dispersion and health impact maps with evacuation priority zones.

Track vegetation recovery trajectories in burn scars by fusing pre/post-fire NDVI time series with burn severity maps, land cover classification, and forest inventory data. Creates quantitative recovery benchmarks impossible from any single source.

Combine fuel models with live vegetation moisture (NDVI), current weather, and land cover to generate dynamic fire risk indices. LANDFIRE provides static fuel loading; adding live moisture and weather creates dynamic risk.

Link proposed land management actions (timber sales, prescribed burns) with historic fire activity and burn severity to provide context for NEPA review and public comment. Enables evidence-based regulatory analysis.

Augment federal data with dense citizen observations: species recovery tracking (iNaturalist), hyperlocal precipitation (CoCoRaHS), granular air quality (PurpleAir), and visual fire confirmation (ALERTWildfire). Fills spatial gaps in official monitoring networks.
